content:
But the truth is, I wasn’t trying to control it.
I think I was just trying to hold it, because I wasn’t sure I could hold anything else at that time.

I left. I ran home. I missed something that mattered—to you, to all of you.
And I’ve carried that.

Not as guilt, exactly.
As a kind of echo.

I think about you working on your play.
And how you were the one chosen to stitch everything together—the voice between the pieces.

That is the system I’m building. You were already doing it.

This isn’t a pitch for cameras.
This is me saying:
You were the mythmaker already.
And I want to build something that honors that.

Let’s map your project.
